notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. RCA at deliver/rca.md is the authoritative specification — user-reviewed and approved 2026-04-25 in Phase 2 before roadmap creation.

Two-step Outside-In TDD: Step 01-01 writes failing regression tests at handler + store layers (RED — committed with --no-verify per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds). Step 01-02 lands the additive trait change, redb per-entry index persistence, snapshot frame v2, handler swap, and existing-test fixups in a single cut (GREEN).

Single-cut migration per user memory `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — no compatibility shims, no deprecated alias, no two-phase trait migration. PutOutcome variants gain commit_index fields; IntentStore::get gains an index in its return tuple; every in-workspace caller updated in the same commit. RaftStore (Phase 2) wires through the same shape; this fix does not block that work.

Wire compatibility: SubmitJobResponse.commit_index and JobDescription.commit_index are u64 fields on the OpenAPI surface — JSON shape unchanged, semantics tightened from 'current store cursor' to 'index at which this entry was written'. Clients receive a more stable value. ClusterStatus.commit_index keeps the store-wide cursor — it correctly documents store-level commit count, not a per-entry index. The OpenAPI schema description text gets an additive clarification but the field type and name stay unchanged.

Testing tier mapping (all Tier 1 — acceptance / unit; no DST / proptest / trybuild in play; this is a contract violation, not a concurrency / input-space / type-system property): integration tests live behind the `integration-tests` feature gate per .claude/rules/testing.md §Integration vs unit gating; new store-layer per-entry tests live alongside `commit_index_monotonic.rs` under `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/` (the default lane — no real-infra, no concurrency).

Storage layout choice (delegated to crafter, document inline): the per-entry index can be persisted as either (a) a parallel `entry_index: &[u8] -> u64` redb table written in the same write txn as `entries`, OR (b) a packed `[u64-LE-index || value]` frame in the existing entries table. Option (a) is cleaner separation but adds a second table to every write txn; option (b) is single-table and arguably more local but requires every reader to slice off the prefix. Crafter chooses based on what makes the snapshot frame v2 simpler. Both are acceptable; document the choice in the redb_backend.rs module docstring.

Snapshot frame v2 — additive bump from v1. The frame magic stays `OSNP`; the version word advances from 1 to 2; rkyv payload schema gains a per-entry `commit_index: u64`. bootstrap_from must accept v1 frames (no per-entry index → assign 0 to every entry) for forward-compatibility with already-written DR backups; export_snapshot always writes v2. This is the ONLY place v1 compatibility is preserved — no other call site accepts v1.

Mutation testing scope: diff-scoped via `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main` per .claude/rules/testing.md §Mutation testing. The wrapper auto-detects --package overdrive-core, --package overdrive-store-local, --package overdrive-control-plane and auto-adds --features integration-tests where declared. Kill-rate gate ≥80% on the diff. Mutants targets: the per-entry index assignment in put / put_if_absent / txn (a mutation that uses the wrong counter value reaches PutOutcome and fails the user-story regression test), the get tuple's index field, and the snapshot frame v2 round-trip.

DST scope: SimObservationStore is unaffected (this is intent-store, not observation). LocalIntentStore IS the sim intent store per .claude/rules/testing.md §Store composition, so any DST test that asserts on commit_index will pick up the new contract automatically. A grep for `commit_index` across `crates/*/tests/` should reveal no DST assertions on per-entry semantics today (only global monotonicity); confirm before GREEN commit.

reviews.scope: RCA + proposed fix direction (trait additive change to PutOutcome and IntentStore::get; per-entry redb persistence; snapshot frame v2; handler swap; user-story regression test)
reviews.notes: User confirmed: (1) root cause match; (2) fix direction approved (additive trait change, single-cut migration); (3) no scope-narrowing constraints — proceed with full proposed fix.

phases.name: Make commit_index per-entry: trait surface + per-entry persistence + handler swap
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 writes the regression tests at handler + store layers that pin the per-entry contract (RED — committed with --no-verify). Step 01-02 lands the cohesive single-cut fix: PutOutcome::Inserted/KeyExists carry commit_index; IntentStore::get returns Option<(Bytes, u64)>; LocalIntentStore persists per-entry indices in redb and bumps-and-captures inside the spawn_blocking write txn; snapshot frame v2 carries indices through export/bootstrap; handlers consume the returned indices; existing tests pattern-match the new variant shapes and assert per-entry semantics. Step 01-01's regression tests flip RED→GREEN within the Step 01-02 commit (test and fix coupled in the reviewer's view).

steps.name: RED regression tests — per-entry commit_index at handler + store layers
steps.description: Write three new failing tests that pin the per-entry commit_index contract. (a) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/per_entry_commit_index.rs` — the user-story scenario verbatim: submit A → capture submit_response.commit_index as idx_a; submit a DIFFERENT spec B → capture submit_response.commit_index as idx_b; describe A → capture describe_response.commit_index as idx_a_after; assert idx_b > idx_a AND idx_a_after == idx_a. This test MUST fail against current HEAD because describe_job returns the live counter (which has advanced past idx_a after B's write). (b) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/per_entry_commit_index.rs` — store-layer test that pins the contract independently of the HTTP layer: put two distinct keys with distinct values; the index returned via the new PutOutcome::Inserted::commit_index for write 1 must be < the index returned for write 2; a subsequent `get(key1)` must return the index from write 1 (not the current store counter). This test will fail to COMPILE against current HEAD because PutOutcome::Inserted has no commit_index field and get returns Option<Bytes> not Option<(Bytes, u64)>. Strategy per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds: commit the test BODY against the future trait shape (the test file references PutOutcome::Inserted { commit_index } and tuple-destructures get's return) — the workspace will not compile until 01-02 lands; commit with --no-verify and explicitly call out the intentional RED in the commit message. The crafter MUST verify the workspace fails to compile in the expected places (a `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` run that errors on the missing PutOutcome field is the proof). (c) Tighten `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/concurrent_submit_toctou.rs:269` `concurrent_byte_identical_submits_return_single_commit_index` — the existing test asserts all responses share the same commit_index but doesn't pin it to the FIRST writer's index. Add a follow-on assertion: re-`describe(job_id)` after the burst and assert `describe_response.commit_index == winning_commit_index`. This will fail at runtime against current HEAD for the same reason as (a). Implementation contract: each test imports the future trait shape; the workspace is intentionally RED at the end of this commit. The commit message uses `git commit --no-verify` and the body explicitly says `RED scaffold per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits — Step 01-02 lands the GREEN trait change and store impl in the next commit; this commit is intentionally non-compiling at the test layer to make the RED visible.` Commit trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`.
steps.criteria: Three test additions/modifications: (1) NEW `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/per_entry_commit_index.rs` with at least one test function `describe_returns_index_at_which_entry_was_written` exercising the submit-A → submit-B → describe-A flow; (2) NEW `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/per_entry_commit_index.rs` with at least two test functions: `put_if_absent_returns_commit_index_assigned_in_txn` and `get_returns_index_at_which_entry_was_written`; (3) MODIFIED `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/concurrent_submit_toctou.rs` — `concurrent_byte_identical_submits_return_single_commit_index` gains a follow-on `describe_round_trip` assertion that the described commit_index equals the winning_commit_index. The new files are wired into their respective `tests/integration.rs` / `tests/acceptance.rs` entrypoints (per .claude/rules/testing.md the integration entrypoint uses an inline `mod integration { ... }` block — append the new module). RED verification: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests -E 'test(per_entry_commit_index)'` and `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(per_entry_commit_index)'` MUST fail (compile error on the store side because PutOutcome variants have no commit_index field; runtime assertion failure on the control-plane side because describe_job returns the live counter). The compile error message must reference `PutOutcome::Inserted` or the missing `commit_index` field — not an unrelated typo. Commit message: `test(intent-store,control-plane): pin per-entry commit_index contract (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit uses `git commit --no-verify` and the commit body explicitly cites .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §WHY 1A — the new `per_entry_commit_index.rs` integration test pins that `describe(A).commit_index` equals the index assigned to A's write, not the live store counter.

RCA §WHY 5B (PutOutcome::Inserted carries no index) — the new store-layer `per_entry_commit_index.rs` test asserts that PutOutcome::Inserted::commit_index reflects the index assigned inside the write txn, distinct between two consecutive distinct-key writes.

RCA §WHY 5C (missing regression test for per-entry semantics) — the user-story scenario from `docs/feature/phase-1-control-plane-core/discuss/user-stories.md:234` is lifted verbatim into the new control-plane test.

Existing `concurrent_byte_identical_submits_return_single_commit_index` (concurrent_submit_toctou.rs:269) gains a tightening assertion: a post-burst `describe(job_id)` returns the same commit_index every concurrent submitter saw; without the fix this fails at runtime because describe reads the live counter.

All three tests live behind the existing `integration-tests` feature gate per .claude/rules/testing.md §Integration vs unit gating — store-layer test under `tests/acceptance/` uses no real infra (just LocalIntentStore + tempfile); control-plane integration test uses the existing `spawn_server()` test helper from `concurrent_submit_toctou.rs` shape.

RED verified: `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` fails with an error referencing the missing `commit_index` field on `PutOutcome::Inserted`; `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(per_entry_commit_index)'` exits non-zero with the per-entry assertion failure (idx_a_after != idx_a).

Commit uses `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds — commit body explicitly references the rule and calls out the intentionally non-compiling state, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
steps.tier: Tier 1 (acceptance / integration — requires integration-tests feature per testing.md)
quality_gates: RED verified at the store layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` errors out on the missing `PutOutcome::Inserted::commit_index` field (NOT on a typo or unrelated import).

RED verified at the control-plane layer: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(per_entry_commit_index)'` exits non-zero with the per-entry assertion failure.
All three test additions live behind the `integration-tests` feature; no new CI wiring; no new Cargo.toml feature; no nextest profile change.

The store-layer test does NOT mutate process-global state — no env-var or cwd writes — so `#[serial_test::serial(env)]` is NOT used. The control-plane integration test follows whatever pattern `concurrent_submit_toctou.rs` already uses.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and explicit `--no-verify` rationale citing .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds.
deliverables: Module declarations added to existing `tests/integration.rs` and `tests/acceptance.rs` entrypoints (inline `mod integration { ... }` / `mod acceptance { ... }` block).
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Proposed fix and the user-stories example at docs/feature/phase-1-control-plane-core/discuss/user-stories.md:234; verify the existing `spawn_server()` helper shape in `concurrent_submit_toctou.rs` (it returns `(handle, bound, tmp, ca_pem)`); verify the `tests/integration.rs` entrypoint uses an inline `mod integration { ... }` block per testing.md §Integration vs unit gating. RED_ACCEPTANCE — write the user-story integration test as the outermost RED; it MUST drive a real `submit_job` → `submit_job` → `describe_job` flow against `spawn_server()`. RED_UNIT — write the store-layer per_entry_commit_index.rs against the FUTURE trait shape; the test references `PutOutcome::Inserted { commit_index }` and tuple-destructures get's return. The workspace will fail to compile in the control-plane crate ONCE 01-02 lands the trait change without updating the existing pattern matches; that is acceptable because 01-02 is responsible for the workspace-wide pattern-match update. For 01-01's commit, the workspace fails to compile at the STORE-CRATE TESTS level (not main code) because the test references the future variant. Verify with `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests`. GREEN — SKIPPED in this step (occurs in 01-02). COMMIT — `git commit --no-verify` with body: `RED scaffold per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits. The store-layer test references the future PutOutcome::Inserted::commit_index field and the future Option<(Bytes, u64)> return shape of IntentStore::get — both land in Step 01-02. The control-plane test fails at runtime because describe_job returns the live store counter; it will flip GREEN in the same 01-02 commit. Step-ID: 01-01`. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. Concurrent_submit_toctou.rs tightening: append a `describe_round_trip` call to the existing test BODY that re-fetches each unique job_id and asserts the returned commit_index equals the recorded winning_commit_index; the existing assertions stay untouched.

steps.name: GREEN cohesive fix — additive trait change + per-entry redb storage + snapshot frame v2 + handler swap + existing-test fixups
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Proposed fix in a single cohesive commit. Production changes: (1) `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/intent_store.rs` — `PutOutcome::Inserted` gains `commit_index: u64`; `PutOutcome::KeyExists` gains `commit_index: u64` (carries the index of the prior write at this key); `IntentStore::get` signature changes from `Result<Option<Bytes>, IntentStoreError>` to `Result<Option<(Bytes, u64)>, IntentStoreError>`. The doc comment on `PutOutcome` and `get` are updated to specify per-entry semantics explicitly. (2) `crates/overdrive-store-local/src/redb_backend.rs` — per-entry index persistence (crafter chooses table layout: parallel `entry_index: &[u8] -> u64` table OR packed `[u64-LE || value]` frame in the existing entries table; document choice in the module docstring); `bump_commit` and the new index assignment happen INSIDE `spawn_blocking` so the index returned via `PutOutcome` is exactly the one assigned in the write transaction that wrote the bytes; `put`, `put_if_absent`, `delete`, and `txn` paths are updated; `get` returns `(Bytes, u64)`; `LocalIntentStore::commit_index()` (the inherent global-cursor accessor on the concrete type, used by `cluster_status`) stays unchanged. (3) `crates/overdrive-store-local/src/snapshot_frame.rs` — frame v2: magic stays `OSNP`; version word advances 1 → 2; rkyv payload schema gains a per-entry `commit_index: u64`; `decode` accepts both v1 (assigning 0 to every entry) and v2; `encode` always emits v2. The frame v2 schema is documented in the module docstring. (4) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/handlers.rs` — `submit_job` consumes `commit_index` from the `PutOutcome::Inserted` and `PutOutcome::KeyExists` variants directly (no fallback to `state.store.commit_index()`); `describe_job` consumes `commit_index` from the new `get` tuple; `cluster_status` keeps `state.store.commit_index()` (correct for store-wide cursor). Test fixups: (5) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/put_if_absent.rs` — pattern-match the new `PutOutcome::Inserted { commit_index }` and `PutOutcome::KeyExists { existing, commit_index }` variants; add per-entry assertions where structurally natural (e.g. concurrent put_if_absent on the same key returns the SAME commit_index in both KeyExists and Inserted projections). (6) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/commit_index_monotonic.rs` — every test that calls `store.commit_index()` after a `get` now also asserts the index returned via `get`'s tuple matches the index assigned at write time; tests that call `put` then assert on `commit_index()` keep their assertions on the global cursor (the inherent accessor still tracks store-wide commits). The existing `fresh_store_has_commit_index_zero`, `put_advances_commit_index_by_exactly_one`, `n_puts_advance_commit_index_by_exactly_n`, `commit_index_advances_once_per_put_stepwise`, `delete_advances_commit_index`, `txn_commit_bumps_commit_index_exactly_once` all still pass against the inherent accessor. (7) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/snapshot_roundtrip.rs` — round-trip assertions verify that per-entry indices survive export → bootstrap_from → export. Add a v1 → v2 compatibility test: a hand-crafted v1 frame bootstraps successfully with all entries assigned index 0. (8) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/snapshot_proptest.rs` — proptest generators produce per-entry indices; round-trip remains byte-identical for v2; a property test asserts that two semantically-equal stores (same keys, values, AND indices) produce byte-identical v2 frames. (9) Step 01-01's three new tests flip RED→GREEN within this commit: the store-layer per_entry_commit_index.rs compiles and passes; the control-plane per_entry_commit_index.rs runtime assertions hold; the tightened concurrent_submit_toctou.rs assertion holds. (10) Doctest sweep: any rustdoc example that pattern-matches PutOutcome or destructures get's return is updated. Commit verification: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` AND `cargo test --doc --workspace` AND `cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` — all green. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: no compatibility shim, no PutOutcome::InsertedV2, no separate `get_with_index`. The trait surface change is a clean break inside the workspace; every caller is updated in this commit. Commit message: `fix(intent-store,control-plane): make commit_index per-entry across PutOutcome and get` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit): (1) PutOutcome variants carry commit_index; IntentStore::get returns Option<(Bytes, u64)>; trait docstrings updated. (2) LocalIntentStore persists per-entry indices in redb (chosen layout documented in module docstring); bump-and-capture happens INSIDE spawn_blocking; PutOutcome::Inserted carries the assigned index; PutOutcome::KeyExists carries the index of the existing entry; get returns the tuple. (3) Snapshot frame v2 carries per-entry indices; decode accepts v1 and v2; encode emits v2 only. (4) handlers.rs: submit_job uses the index from PutOutcome variants directly; describe_job uses the index from get's tuple; cluster_status keeps the inherent global cursor. Test verification: (5) Step 01-01's three tests flip RED → GREEN within this commit. (6) All pre-existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` exits 0. (7) A new snapshot_roundtrip test asserts v1 frames bootstrap with index 0 (forward-compat); a new test asserts v2 frames round-trip indices losslessly. (8) `concurrent_submit_toctou.rs::concurrent_byte_identical_submits_return_single_commit_index` passes against the new code: the byte-identical concurrent submitters all return the same commit_index AND a follow-on describe returns that same commit_index. Single-cut migration: no compatibility shim; no PutOutcome::InsertedV2; no `get_with_index` method. The doctest in api.rs / handlers.rs (if any) is updated to reflect per-entry semantics. Commit message: `fix(intent-store,control-plane): make commit_index per-entry across PutOutcome and get` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`). Post-GREEN smoke check: `git diff --name-only` shows the full set of production and test files modified; the diff includes the trait change, the store impl, the snapshot frame, the handlers, and the test fixups all in one commit.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Proposed fix steps 1-5 — trait additive change, per-entry redb persistence (with bump-and-capture inside spawn_blocking), snapshot frame v2, handler swap, and test fixups all land in a single cohesive commit.

RCA §WHY 5A (trait-surface gap closure) — IntentStore::get returns the per-entry index in its tuple; PutOutcome::Inserted and KeyExists each carry a commit_index field; the trait surface now structurally supports the per-entry contract.

RCA §WHY 5B (PutOutcome::Inserted carries no index — closure) — the index assigned inside the redb write txn is captured and returned via PutOutcome::Inserted::commit_index; no read of state.store.commit_index() exists in the submit_job::Inserted arm.
Step 01-01's regression tests transition RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (proving the fix is load-bearing and the tests pin the invariant).

Snapshot frame v2 carries per-entry indices through export → bootstrap_from → export; v1 frames are accepted by bootstrap_from with all entries assigned index 0 (forward compat for any DR backups already on disk in v1 format); export_snapshot always emits v2.
All pre-existing tests remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: no compatibility shim, no deprecated alias, no two-phase trait migration; every in-workspace caller of PutOutcome and IntentStore::get is updated in this commit.

ClusterStatus.commit_index continues to read the store-wide global cursor (correct semantics for that endpoint); only SubmitJobResponse.commit_index and JobDescription.commit_index switch to per-entry semantics.

`cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` green on the touched crates; typed errors per .claude/rules/development.md (no new `eyre::Report` on library boundaries; no new `.expect()` outside binary entrypoints).
redb table layout choice (parallel index table OR packed value frame) is documented in `crates/overdrive-store-local/src/redb_backend.rs` module docstring; rationale stated.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
quality_gates: Workspace compiles green: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests` exits 0.
Full test suite green: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0.
Step 01-01's three tests transition RED → GREEN without modification.
Clippy clean: `cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` on overdrive-core, overdrive-store-local, overdrive-control-plane.

Single-cut: no PutOutcome::InsertedV2, no `get_with_index`, no `#[deprecated]` aliases — `git grep -E '(InsertedV2|get_with_index|#\[deprecated\])' crates/overdrive-{core,store-local,control-plane}` returns no matches.
redb backend module docstring documents the per-entry index storage choice (parallel table vs packed frame).
Snapshot frame v2: a v1 → v2 forward-compat test exists; a v2 → v2 round-trip lossless test exists.
Bump-and-capture happens INSIDE spawn_blocking: a grep on redb_backend.rs shows no `self.bump_commit()` call OUTSIDE a `spawn_blocking` body for the put / put_if_absent / txn paths.
deliverables: Cohesive single commit landing the trait change, store impl, snapshot frame v2, handler swap, and test fixups.
Updated rustdoc on PutOutcome and IntentStore::get specifying per-entry semantics.
redb_backend.rs module docstring documents the chosen per-entry index storage layout and rationale.
Snapshot frame v2 documented in snapshot_frame.rs module docstring (magic, version word, payload schema, v1 → v2 compat behaviour).
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Proposed fix; verify the chosen redb layout is consistent with the existing snapshot_frame.rs schema; confirm no other call site of IntentStore::get exists outside the four files listed (a `git grep 'fn get' crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/intent_store.rs` and `git grep '\.store\.get(' crates/` sweep); confirm no other PutOutcome consumer exists outside handlers.rs and the store tests. RED_ACCEPTANCE — Step 01-01 already wrote the outermost RED tests; verify they currently fail/don't compile. RED_UNIT — for any new internal helper introduced inside redb_backend.rs (e.g. an `InsertedRow` struct that bundles bytes and index, or a `decode_entry` helper), write a same-crate unit test that pins the helper's behaviour BEFORE writing it; commit_index_monotonic.rs is a strong existing harness — extend it to assert the new per-entry projection. GREEN — implement the trait change first (intent_store.rs), then the store impl (redb_backend.rs), then the snapshot frame (snapshot_frame.rs), then the handlers (handlers.rs), then the test fixups; at each layer verify with `cargo check -p <crate>` before moving on. Run the full nextest suite at the end of GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests`. The bump-and-capture inside spawn_blocking is the load-bearing detail — read carefully: today the put_if_absent body inside spawn_blocking returns `(outcome, emit)` and `bump_commit()` runs outside; the new shape captures the new index INSIDE the closure (where the redb commit happens) and bakes it into PutOutcome::Inserted::commit_index before the closure returns. The `bump_commit()` call moves inside the closure too, so the inherent global counter and the per-entry index are bumped together inside the same atomic operation — no possible inversion. COMMIT — `git commit` (no --no-verify); the commit body explicitly states the trait change, the per-entry storage choice, the frame v2 bump, and the handler swap; the message body links to deliver/rca.md. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`. Post-commit smoke check: run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` one more time to confirm zero regressions across the full workspace; this is the input to the post-merge integration gate (Phase 3.5).
